excluded_defs.name: SeverityProvider
excluded_defs.reason: read to confirm the property signature; not changed

exploration_log.aha_moment: The existing WithSeverity overloads on IRuleBuilderOptions already call Configurable(rule).Current.SeverityProvider. Since IRuleBuilderOptionsConditions also extends IRuleBuilder, the same Configurable(IRuleBuilder<T,TProperty>) helper works. So the fix is just adding 4 mirrored methods with the new receiver type.
exploration_log.hindsight: Nothing to change - direct mirror of existing overloads onto the new interface.

alternatives: add a single catch-all extension on IRuleBuilder<T,TProperty>
key_decisions.decision: Add 4 explicit overloads mirroring the existing IRuleBuilderOptions overloads
key_decisions.reasoning: Explicit overloads are consistent with the existing API surface; a catch-all on IRuleBuilder would broaden the API too much

queries.query_text: Add WithSeverity(Severity), WithSeverity(Func<T,Severity>), WithSeverity(Func<T,TProperty,Severity>), WithSeverity(Func<T,TProperty,ValidationContext,Severity>) overloads for IRuleBuilderOptionsConditions in DefaultValidatorOptions.cs

solve_notes: Added four `WithSeverity` overloads in `DefaultValidatorOptions.cs` targeting `IRuleBuilderOptionsConditions<T, TProperty>`, mirroring the existing four overloads for `IRuleBuilderOptions`. Each overload sets `Configurable(rule).Current.SeverityProvider` using the same lambda patterns as the existing overloads.
task_text:
The `WithSeverity` extension methods in `DefaultValidatorOptions.cs`
target `IRuleBuilderOptions<T, TProperty>` only. Rules that use
`Must()` or `Custom()` return `IRuleBuilderOptionsConditions<T, TProperty>`,
leaving those rules unable to call `WithSeverity()` without an explicit
cast. Add mirroring `WithSeverity(Severity)`, `WithSeverity(Func<T, Severity>)`,
`WithSeverity(Func<T, TProperty, Severity>)`, and
`WithSeverity(Func<T, TProperty, ValidationContext<T>, Severity>)` overloads
on `IRuleBuilderOptionsConditions<T, TProperty>` in `DefaultValidatorOptions.cs`,
storing the severity provider in `RuleComponent` via the existing
`SeverityProvider` path.
